titleTag: Illinois Landlord Tenant Law 2026 | Rights, Deposits & Eviction
metaDescription: Illinois landlord tenant law 2026: security deposit limits, eviction notice requirements, tenant rights & more. Plain-language guide for new landlords.

sections.heading: Security deposit rules
body: If you're looking for Illinois security deposit rules, focus on (1) whether there is a cap, (2) when the deposit must be returned, and (3) whether an itemized statement is required.
bullets: Deposit cap: 3 months rent
Return deadline: 30 days after move-out/tenancy end (deductions can affect details).
Itemized statement: required if deductions are taken (check deadlines and delivery method).
Deductions: Varies — confirm current statute.
Penalty risk: Varies — confirm current statute.
Interest: Varies — confirm current statute.

sections.heading: Eviction notice requirements
body: For Illinois eviction notice requirements, the notice period often depends on why the eviction is being pursued (nonpayment vs lease violation vs holdover). Always use the correct notice form and follow the court process.
bullets: Nonpayment notice: commonly 30 days (verify by tenancy type/notice form).

Nonpayment notice type: 735 ILCS 5/9-210. OTHER LEASE VIOLATIONS. For violations other than nonpayment of rent, the landlord may serve a written notice specifying the nature of the violation and demanding remedy or surrender within 10 DAYS of service.
Lease violation notice: commonly 30 days (may allow cure).

Lease violation notice type: 735 ILCS 5/9-210. OTHER LEASE VIOLATIONS. For violations other than nonpayment of rent, the landlord may serve a written notice specifying the nature of the violation and demanding remedy or surrender within 10 DAYS of service.
Cure allowed: varies
Month-to-month termination: commonly 5 days (verify tenancy type).
Holdover/termination notice type: 735 ILCS 5/9-207. HOLDOVER/MONTH-TO-MONTH TERMINATION.
Court process: confirm whether judicial eviction is required (most states prohibit self-help).
Self-help eviction: varies — confirm current statute and case law.
Timeline:
Court steps: ILLINOIS EVICTION NOTICE REQUIREMENTS:

sections.heading: Landlord entry notice
body: If you're searching for Illinois landlord entry notice rules, look for (1) required notice time, (2) allowed entry reasons, and (3) emergency exceptions.
bullets: Notice: often governed by lease terms and a reasonableness standard (state law may not set a single hour-count).
Typical notice time: varies; many leases use 24 hours as a practical baseline.
Allowed reasons: (a) The landlord shall have the right of access to the dwelling unit at reasonable times with the consent of the tenant for the purpose of making inspections, alterations or repairs.
Timing: (a) The landlord shall have the right of access to the dwelling unit at reasonable times with the consent of the tenant for the purpose of making inspections, alterations or repairs.
Emergencies: generally allow entry without advance notice.
Tenant refusal: varies
Remedies/penalties: Varies — confirm remedies (injunction, damages, attorney fees) under state law.

sections.heading: Rent increase rules
body: For Illinois rent increase rules, most states focus on notice requirements, while rent caps are usually local (or limited to specific covered housing).
bullets: Statewide rent control: typically none, but local/covered-housing rules may apply.
Local rent control: may exist in certain cities/counties (check if your property is covered).
Notice of rent increase: varies — confirm current statute and any local ordinances.
Fixed-term leases: Varies — confirm your lease terms and statute.
Month-to-month: Varies — generally allowed with proper notice.
Delivery: Varies — confirm written notice and delivery method requirements.
Just cause: varies
Retaliation: Retaliation protections may limit rent increases after protected tenant complaints; confirm local/state rules.

sections.heading: Tenant rights & habitability basics
body: Most Illinois landlord tenant law disputes involve habitability, repairs, and retaliation protections. Keep repair requests documented and respond promptly.
bullets: Habitability: implied warranty generally applies
Landlord repair duty: Varies — confirm landlord repair/habitability duties in statute and local code.
Tenant notice: If landlord fails to repair condition within 14 days of written notice that endangers health or safety, tenant in a building of 5 or more units may:
Repair-and-deduct: varies
Rent withholding: varies
Termination: Varies — some states allow termination after notice and failure to repair.
Retaliation: varies
Code enforcement: Varies — contact local housing/code enforcement (city/county) for inspections and official repair orders.

jurisdictionNotes: Local ordinances (city/county) can be stricter than state law.
Rules can change; verify before acting on any single requirement.
confidenceNotes: This page is being refreshed for accuracy; key limits and notice periods are verified state-by-state during the rollout.
